problem: The application needs a changeable list of favorite restaurants. No restaurant, favorite, or list-management concept exists in the codebase today. The only data feature is a demo counter with a single DB table. A new domain feature is required: database schema, API routes, Zod validation, client/server UI components, i18n strings in both locales, and a database migration, all following the established counter-feature pattern.

files.why: Central Drizzle ORM schema file. Currently defines only `counterSchema` (pgTable with serial PK, integer count, timestamps). New restaurant/favorites table(s) must be added here. Referenced by drizzle.config.ts as the single schema source.
Drizzle Kit config pointing to src/models/Schema.ts for schema and ./migrations for output. After schema changes, `npm run db:generate` produces a new migration SQL file.
Existing migration creating the counter table. New migration(s) will be auto-generated alongside this by drizzle-kit.
Creates Drizzle DB connection with `import * as schema from '@/models/Schema'`. Wildcard import means new schema exports from Schema.ts are automatically available without changes here.
Global cached Drizzle singleton instance. Import point for all data access in API routes and server components. No changes needed.

Reference API route pattern: Zod safeParse validation, Drizzle insert with onConflictDoUpdate, NextResponse.json, logger usage, x-e2e-random-id header for test isolation. New restaurant API routes should follow this structure.
Reference Zod validation pattern (z.object with field constraints). New restaurant validation schema should follow this in src/validations/.
Reference client component pattern: 'use client' directive, react-hook-form with zodResolver, useTranslations for i18n, fetch() to API route, router.refresh() for server data revalidation.
Reference async server component pattern: direct db.query usage with drizzle-orm eq(), getTranslations for i18n, headers() for request context.
Shows how to access authenticated user identity via `currentUser()` from '@clerk/nextjs/server'. Essential pattern for user-scoped data.
Dashboard page pattern: DashboardPageProps type, generateMetadata with locale, setRequestLocale, default export. Favorites page likely belongs as a sibling under (auth)/dashboard/.
Dashboard layout with navigation links rendered via BaseTemplate leftNav/rightNav. A new favorites page needs a nav link entry added here.
Auth layout wrapping ClerkProvider. All pages in the (auth) route group are behind Clerk authentication, providing user identity.
English i18n strings organized by namespace (component/page name). New namespace and keys needed for all restaurant feature UI text.
French i18n strings. Must receive corresponding translations for every new key added to en.json. check:i18n script validates completeness.
Type-safe env validation with @t3-oss/env-nextjs. DATABASE_URL and CLERK_SECRET_KEY already validated. No new env vars expected for this feature.
App configuration with locale list (en, fr) and prefix strategy. No changes expected but defines i18n boundaries.
Shared layout template with leftNav/rightNav prop slots. Dashboard layout passes navigation links through this.
Exports locale-aware Link, usePathname, useRouter from next-intl. Used by client components for navigation.
Reference page composition: imports server + client components, generateMetadata with locale namespace, useTranslations for page-level content.
Scripts and quality gates: lint, check:types, check:deps, check:i18n, test, test:e2e, build-local, db:generate, db:migrate.
Pre-commit hooks: ultracite lint/fix and knip dependency check. Commit-msg hook runs commitlint for conventional commits.
Mandatory coding standards: named exports only, absolute imports via @/, Zod type-only imports, no useMemo/useCallback, Tailwind v4, conventional commits, test naming, JSDoc requirements.

facts: No restaurant, favorite, or list-management code exists anywhere in the codebase.
The only existing DB table is 'counter' with columns: id (serial PK), count (integer default 0), updated_at (timestamp), created_at (timestamp).
Drizzle ORM v0.45.1 with PostgreSQL dialect is the database layer; schema defined in src/models/Schema.ts using pgTable().
DBConnection.ts uses `import * as schema from '@/models/Schema'` so new schema exports are auto-available without code changes.
Database migrations are generated via `npm run db:generate` (drizzle-kit generate) and applied via `npm run db:migrate`.
Authentication is handled by Clerk v7.0.6. The (auth) route group wraps pages in ClerkProvider.
User identity is accessible server-side via `currentUser()` from '@clerk/nextjs/server' (used in src/components/Hello.tsx).
The counter feature demonstrates the complete vertical slice: Schema.ts -> CounterValidation.ts -> api/counter/route.ts -> CounterForm.tsx (client) + CurrentCount.tsx (server).
API routes return NextResponse.json(); Zod validation errors return status 422 with z.treeifyError().
The counter API route uses an upsert pattern (insert + onConflictDoUpdate) with .returning().
Server components read data directly via db.query (e.g., db.query.counterSchema.findFirst()).
Client components use 'use client' directive, react-hook-form with zodResolver, and fetch() to API routes.
After API mutations, client components call router.refresh() to trigger server component revalidation.
i18n uses next-intl with two locales: 'en' and 'fr'. Server: getTranslations(); Client: useTranslations(). All user-visible strings must be in both locale files.
Styling uses Tailwind CSS v4 utility classes on plain HTML elements. No component library (shadcn, Radix, MUI, etc.).
AGENTS.md mandates: named exports only (except Next.js pages), absolute imports via @/, no useMemo/useCallback, Zod type-only imports, conventional commits.

Quality gates: ultracite lint (type-aware), TypeScript strict mode (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, noImplicitReturns), vitest unit tests, Playwright E2E, knip unused deps, check:i18n for locale completeness.
Pre-commit hooks via lefthook: commitlint for commit messages, ultracite fix for lint/format, knip for unused deps.
The counter feature is in the (marketing) route group (public). A user-specific favorites feature should be in the (auth) route group (protected).
Next.js 16.2.1 with React 19.2.4 and React Compiler enabled in production.
The dashboard layout renders navigation via BaseTemplate with leftNav/rightNav props (currently: Dashboard, User Profile links).
No existing user-scoped data pattern in the DB (counter is global, not tied to a user ID).
unknowns: Whether 'changeable list' implies full CRUD (create, read, update, delete) or a simpler add/remove pattern.
Whether the favorite restaurants list should be user-scoped (per authenticated Clerk user) or global. 'My favorite restaurants' strongly implies per-user, but is not confirmed.
What data fields a restaurant entry should have (name only, or also address, cuisine type, rating, notes, URL, etc.).
Whether restaurants should be free-form entries the user creates, or selections from a predefined catalog.
Whether list ordering/reordering is part of the 'changeable' requirement.
Whether the feature needs its own dedicated dashboard sub-page or should be embedded in the existing dashboard page.
How Clerk user IDs should be stored in the DB schema (text column, varchar, etc.) since no user-scoping pattern exists to reference.
What the French translations should be for new i18n keys.
Whether there are constraints on the number of favorites a user can have.
